repo_prior_art.cite: none; the closest catalogue Stat entries concern fixed-dose minimax and weak-event ratios, not an illness-death planned-hazard EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased derives an EIF for a closely related smoothed illness-death intervention; WestlingLuedtkeGilbertCarone2024Survival and KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017ContinuousDR supply adjacent survival/continuous-treatment EIF templates.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference / “bounded-count-diameter” has a rare-event causal-ratio obstruction, not a CAR illness-death marked-Poisson score experiment.

published_prior_art.cite: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous gives effective-sample-size theory for exponentially tilted static exposures; AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment and ColangeloLee2023ContinuousDML give ordinary continuous-treatment Gaussian inference, not this finite-count multistate point-process limit.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference / “bounded-count-diameter” is a close honest-diameter analogue, but for PN/PS ratio shells rather than localized initiation-time survival risk.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI provides Hölder bias-aware CI geometry, while MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased and AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment remain in ordinary fixed/shrinking-window Gaussian settings; none gives this CAR critical-law diameter frontier.

novelty_flags.label: related_work, comparator_promise_table, and thm:exogenous-eif gap
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard arXiv version places its relevant EIF result in Theorem 3.1, not Theorem 4.1; correct the anchor while retaining the informed-by comparison.

soundness_flags.label: lem:extension-invariance and target_estimand
soundness_flags.one_line: A Hölder representative agreeing only f_{U,P}-a.e. is not uniquely determined at u_star unless f_{U,P} has local full support; add ass:conditional-initiation-positivity (or an explicit positive-density-near-u_star condition) to the lemma before defining theta(P) from that representative.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-validity, ass:car, and ass:compatible-extension
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The causal reading is conditional on compatible full-data extensions satisfying the displayed intervention-validity and censoring restrictions; these are assumed rather than identified from P.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The exact initiation-time target and all causal interpretation are asserted only conditional on P_full in mathfrak E(P); the proposed critical experiment remains an observed-law statistical claim.

source_verification_receipts.locator: §3.1, Theorem 3.1; Appendix, “Proof of Theorem 3.1” following Lemma 6.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper studies a stochastic intervention modifying the illness-death no-treatment-to-initiation hazard and derives the relevant interventional-risk EIF as Theorem 3.1; the inspected primary version has no relevant Theorem 4.1 anchor.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair the Martinussen theorem locator and add local positive f_U support to lem:extension-invariance, making the version-selection route to theta(P) explicit.

Sharpen oeq:honest-diameter into a named critical-law-calibrated interval construction with an explicit diameter-rate or lower-bound target, and state the nuisance-rate class on which it is intended to be honest.

Develop the marked-Poisson result into a matched phase diagram: prove the diverging-count Gaussian regime and the bounded-count limit for the same score, then compare their calibrated interval diameters over a common Hölder illness-death class.

recommended_next_step: Revise the target-version support condition and the Martinussen citation anchor, then retain this field-tier phase-transition angle for Stage 0.
